surfaceV.scope_label: Surface spec — Category Board desktop (D3JA0i): 3 scoped sections + coverage checklist
surfaceV.summary: Verified the category-board.md surface spec (3 sections: App Shell & Nav Chrome; MissionDetailHeader/CategoryGroupHeader/Content list; TaskCard state-matrix) against the authoritative screen extract D3JA0i__category-board-desktop.json (token refs resolved via 00-variables.json). The spec is unusually high-fidelity: every token reference, literal text string, enumerated state/variant, and the bulk of px/padding/gap values check out against real node ids. No high-severity build-breaking errors found. The discrepancies are medium/low: (1) the spec cites StatTile Val node ids (Au6r1/OGtWF/BPeON/f5unP) and group GLabel/GCount ids only implicitly and a few cited helper-text ids are absent from the cited tables but those it does cite are correct; (2) two genuinely imprecise/unsupported micro-claims — the DONE StatTile and ProgressBar 'Done' both use $success but the spec never flags that DONE-stat and Done-segment share a token while progress 'remaining' has no node (correct, already noted); (3) the §4 IN_PROGRESS glyph is correctly 9px/normal; (4) the header '~56px' height and '1040px main' are derivations, labelled approximate. The one real FIDELITY nit: spec §2.1 Header says 'no explicit gap → tokens sit flush' for the Wordmark (g3lFfu) — the node indeed has no gap, supported. Net: all three sections are MATCH or near-match; remaining items are low/medium precision notes. Counts reflect per-finding severities across sections.

surfaceV.scope_label: SURFACE spec — Timeline view (desktop), board a3Dgz (timeline.md vs a3Dgz__timeline-desktop.json)
surfaceV.summary: Verified the SURFACE spec for board a3Dgz against its authoritative screen extract in both directions (design->spec coverage and spec->design fidelity). The spec is overwhelmingly accurate: every frame id, literal text string, token reference, layout px/padding/gap, enumerated state/variant, and interaction I spot-checked resolves correctly against the JSON node tree and 00-variables.json. One HIGH-severity factual defect: the StatusCycleButton instance count in §5.1 is wrong/internally inconsistent — the spec claims 6 'empty + border-hover' boxes but the board has 7 (lines 1620,1834,1960,2163,2289,2492,2695), and the table's per-row instance counts sum to 10 rather than the 11 cards the spec itself asserts. Everything else (token hexes, all 11 card instances, all 6 buckets, DaysPill/Pill variant tables, CategoryTag colors+icons, dimmed/done treatments, progress bar 180/180/fill, ViewTabs underline tokens, NavCard active/inactive, stat tiles, target line) matches the screen. A few MEDIUM/LOW items are imprecision rather than wrong facts.

surfaceV.summary: Adversarial bidirectional verification of docs/tech-spec/03-surfaces/mobile.md against the authoritative screen extract h9YSWg__mobile-screens.json (tokens resolved via 00-variables.json). The spec is unusually high-fidelity: I spot-checked every token reference, every literal text string, every enumerated state/variant, all 13 TaskCard instances, all layout px/padding/gap values, the FAB effect, and all node ids — and found essentially no build-breaking discrepancies. All 47 node ids cited in the spec exist in the extract (no invented ids). All arithmetic (318px inner, 128px remaining segment, 20px FAB inset) is correct. Variant/state counts (not-started x6, done x4, blocked x2, closed x1; pill CLOSING x3 / OPEN x3 / DONE x4 / BLOCKED x2 / WINDOW CLOSED x1) match the tree exactly. Group order, counts (1/3,2/3,0/2,1/3,0/2) and the 4/13 cross-check all verify. The main defensible findings are unverifiable claims that reach OUTSIDE this extract: (1) the §7 divergence ledger and coverage checklist cite canonical-component node ids (Ie7mv, HLa60, lLfcN, d4KRE9, AtbGz, d2mdF, db8Tm) and their property values that do NOT exist in this board's extract and cannot be confirmed from the authoritative screen here; (2) interaction/a11y claims (RETRY reset/re-fetch, hover $primary-hover, focus $ring, role=alert) are not encodable in a static node tree and have no on-screen evidence on this board. surfaceV.scope_label: Surface spec NMzE5 — Account · Settings · Legal · Manual (4 scoped sections)
surfaceV.summary: Verified the SURFACE spec for board NMzE5 against its design extract in both directions (design→spec coverage and spec→design fidelity), spot-checking layout px/padding/gap, every cited token reference, literal text strings, enumerated states/variants, and interactions across all four scoped sections (Account & Auth, Settings & Debug, Shake-to-Report, Legal & Manual). The spec is exceptionally accurate: virtually every cited node id, px value, token, literal string, and state matches the extract exactly (Sign In default/error cards, Account profile/DangerZone, Delete Account/History confirm+done states with all counts 64/310/820/1,204/3,902, Settings Switch ON/OFF, FeatureFlagChips, ErrorLog table cell widths 78/56/86/fill, Shake composer/toasts, Legal callouts, Manual TOC + Live WindowState pills). One substantive discrepancy found: the Success-state toast overlay scrim is #0a0a0c80 in the design (identical to the Error overlay), but the spec characterizes #0a0a0c80 as an Error-only "lighter scrim" and (in open items) implies Success uses the heavier #0a0a0ce6 — a mischaracterization of the Success scrim. A few low-severity notes: the Account-area token table and Shake token list omit $accent/$popover even though the Shake spec cites them in-line; the spec says the Account Management area frame has "id not on area" but the frame (D9bEi4) does carry an id. No high-severity build-breaking errors were found.

compV.scope_label: Component contracts — 02-atoms-form.md (Cmp·TextInput, Textarea, Select, SearchField, Checkbox, Radio, Switch)
compV.summary: Verified all 7 form-atom contracts against canonical defs (components-batch1.json), the RcvKu showcase matrices, NMzE5 product screens, the _triage-raw drift notes, scaffolding-plan.md, and the camp-404 source files. Overall the contracts are high-fidelity: TextInput, SearchField, Checkbox and Radio are accurate, and the camp-404/shadcn mappings all resolve (input.tsx/input-field.tsx/textarea.tsx/select.tsx/checkbox.tsx/switch.tsx all exist in camp-404; no camp radio confirmed). The two real defects are on SWITCH (the contract claims "11 standalone toggles in SettingsRow" and "all 11 screen instances confirm the SQUARE cornerRadius-2 track" — but NMzE5 has exactly 7 square Settings switches + 4 PILL switches (id NYAsE/tfit5/JkQ33/Jd7oZ: cornerRadius 12, width 42, no stroke) in the ReportComposer/ShakeReport Toggle Rows, so both the count breakdown and the "all 11 confirm square" claim are false and the pill rendering is uncaptured) and on TEXTAREA/SELECT coverage (the only real product Textarea — ReportComposer Description Field id TVfNR — renders the value in DM Sans 14 / lineHeight 1.45 / padding 12, contradicting the contract's "JetBrains Mono 14 is authoritative, DM Sans is a showcase artifact"; and the only product Selects — XGS3g/SQZnJ — render at h34 / JBMono 12-500 / padding [0,10] / chevron 14, not the contract's 40px / JBMono 14 / [0,12] / chevron 16). Several spec additions (Checkbox indeterminate, SearchField with-clear x, Select error/destructive) are not found anywhere in the design but are self-disclosed as reconciliation additions — flagged low.

Several minor wording/example mismatches (Dep line font size, blocked icon sourcing, Deps button-swap claim). compV.scope_label: Voice & AI component contracts (docs/tech-spec/02-components/04-voice-ai.md) — 17 contracts verified vs canonical defs (components-batch2/4.json) and screens (GZ7xA, T2BChB, RcvKu showcase)
compV.summary: Verified all 17 voice/AI contracts in both directions against canonical defs (components-batch2.json + components-batch4.json) and authoritative screens (GZ7xA §01-04, T2BChB, RcvKu showcase). Canonical anatomies are reproduced with high fidelity — IDs, fills, icons, fontSizes/weights, padding, gaps and literal content (including the MinimizedJobPill arc spinner innerRadius0.62/startAngle60/sweepAngle280 + shadow, the 40-bar canonical waveform, the AINotesBlock 5 numbered steps, the ErrorStateCard 8-cell union) all check out. Reconciliation/widening calls (screen-wins) are policy-correct and not flagged. The most significant defect is RecordingPanel: the spec calls the GZ7xA Voice Capture transcript 'DM Sans 16' four separate times, but the screen (node d6h84) renders it in JetBrains Mono 16 — a build would pick the wrong font. Secondary issues: VoiceFAB processing glyph stated as 'loader-circle' but the authoritative T2BChB screen (and triage) use 'loader'; SourceRow favicon-tone mapping in the spec is wrong relative to the actual §03 sources and its 'failed' variant is structurally divergent from the SourceRow molecule; minor wording/icon mismatches elsewhere. No invented props/variants/tokens of consequence were found; camp-404/shadcn mappings match scaffolding-plan.md.

design_to_spec_gaps.element: ~40%-alpha hue stroke on tint demo frames (e.g. JNawK stroke '#ff6b3566', f41OQ '#d9a73e66', AEhEs '#5ae0a066', strokeWidth 1 on all six demo frames)
design_to_spec_gaps.evidence: Every 12% and 18% tint demo frame carries a 1px hue stroke at alpha suffix '66' (~40%). Spec §5 only enumerates the 1f (12%), 2e (18%), and 0f (~6% CrossRef) alpha steps and says left-accent borders use the solid token; the ~40% (66) border alpha on tinted surfaces is not documented as a convention.

items.notes: Header L W0lEt 'SPACING SCALE' (JetBrains Mono 12/700/+2, fill $primary) + D ayv90 '4px base grid — gaps and padding step in multiples of 4.' verbatim. All 8 steps verified with Bar width = step value: S4 eB3rr w4, S8 QLubA w8, S12 P7Nf0 w12, S16 dQi0k w16, S20 OZBOI w20, S24 Bn5Cf w24, S32 tfbfi w32, S48 LKrtV w48. 40px is skipped between 32 and 48 as the spec notes.

items.item: Foundational primitives (SectionHead, CrossRef, Eyebrow, Swatch, Specimen, Divider, Dot, Pill)
items.notes: SectionHead pattern verified on type/font/colour/elevation/tint blocks: 8x8 $primary Tick (e.g. Jl7EQ) + mono Label JetBrains Mono 13/700/+2 $foreground (e.g. k6aRB 'TYPE SCALE') + 1px $border fill_container Rule (e.g. szfvA) — matches spec §7. CrossRef F4CSDV: fill #ff6b350f, left stroke $primary 2px, padding 14, gap 11, $primary Lucide icon, title DM Sans 14/600 + body 13/normal $muted-foreground — matches. Note: the column-intro header aBlxN 'FOUNDATIONS' is JetBrains Mono 18/700/+2 (not the 13px block SectionHead); spec §7 references the 13px block heads, which is correct.

spec_to_design_unsupported.claim: Spec §7: 'SectionHead — every foundation block opens with this. An 8x8 $primary square Tick + a mono uppercase Label ... + a 1px $border horizontal Rule.'
spec_to_design_unsupported.evidence: Not true for the Spacing block: its SectionHead dSNVF has NO Tick square and NO Rule — it is just label W0lEt 'SPACING SCALE' (fill $primary, not $foreground; size 12 not 13) + description D ayv90. So 'every foundation block opens with [Tick+Label+Rule]' is an overgeneralization. (Spec §6 separately documents the actual spacing header correctly, so internally inconsistent but low impact.)
